tsTroubleshooting.domains: (topic tsTroubleshooting) (topic hi-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic ut-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic sw-d) (topic ui-d) (topic oce-d) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c tsTroubleshooting oceTroubleshooting-c)
tsTroubleshooting.title: 21947 Non volatile memory (NVRAM) corruption detected and handled

abstract.p: Non volatile memory (NVRAM) corruption detected and handled.

This warning is reported when the real-time software detects that the contents of the NVRAM chip located on the Colorado PCI-card are corrupt. This is normal in case the NVRAM has never been written to, but abnormal for all other situations. If this warning is reported, counters are lost and the print quality may be greatly reduced. To fix this you may have to restore an earlier system backup.
Note that it is by no means expected that NVRAM becomes corrupted. In fact, it should never happen after the initial occurrence. If the frequency of occurrence is too high, action is needed by R&D.

tsCauses.p: Please ignore, there is no action required.
